description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who has just received three new application releases.  
    The raw release-note files have already been placed for you under  
        /home/user/deploy/releases/  

        ├── v2.3.1/release_notes.md  
        ├── v2.4.0/release_notes.md  
        └── v2.4.1/release_notes.md  

    Your job is to transform those Markdown files into two machine-readable artefacts and one very small status log.  
    The whole job should take roughly 5–10 terminal commands.

    1. Create the directory /home/user/deploy/logs/ if it does **not** already exist.  
    2. From every *.md* file directly under /home/user/deploy/releases/*/ you must extract:  
       • the semantic version (folder name, e.g. v2.4.1)  
       • the release date (contained on the line that starts with “Date: ”)  
       • the headline (the very first bullet line, without the leading “- ”)  

    3. Use that information to build this plain-text file:  
       File path: /home/user/deploy/logs/deployment_change_log.log  

       Required exact format (newline-delimited, **no header line**):  
           [VERSION] | [DATE] | [HEADLINE]  

       • One entry per release.  
       • Entries must be sorted from newest version to oldest (v2.4.1 first, v2.3.1 last).  
       • Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline so test scripts can `cat` it safely.

    4. Generate a compact JSON summary file at  
       /home/user/deploy/logs/deployment_summary.json  

       Required exact JSON (all on one line, no extra whitespace):  
           {"versions":["v2.4.1","v2.4.0","v2.3.1"],"latest":"v2.4.1"}  

       Pay attention to the order of the array and the lack of spaces.

    5. When everything is complete, write a one-line status marker to  
       /home/user/deploy/logs/verification_report.txt  

       Exact content (including capitalisation and colon):  
           TRANSFORMATION STATUS: SUCCESS  

       This file should also end with a newline.

    The automated checker will look for:  
       • the existence of /home/user/deploy/logs/  
       • correct file names as specified above  
       • exact textual content and order in each file  
       • the trailing newline at the end of each file  

    Do not remove or overwrite any other files. You do not need root privileges.  
    Feel free to display intermediate console output for your own debugging; only the final files will be graded.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task starts:

    1. /home/user/deploy/releases/v2.3.1/release_notes.md  
       Contents:  
       ```
       # Release v2.3.1
       Date: 2023-04-17
       - Fixed issue with authentication timeout
       - Improved logging around API errors
       ```

    2. /home/user/deploy/releases/v2.4.0/release_notes.md  
       Contents:  
       ```
       # Release v2.4.0
       Date: 2023-05-29
       - Added support for PostgreSQL 14
       - Deprecated legacy report generator
       ```

    3. /home/user/deploy/releases/v2.4.1/release_notes.md  
       Contents:  
       ```
       # Release v2.4.1
       Date: 2023-06-12
       - Hotfix: Corrected SQL migration script for user_profiles table
       ```

    Expected files the agent must create:

    A. /home/user/deploy/logs/deployment_change_log.log  
       Exact contents (including final newline):  
       ```
       v2.4.1 | 2023-06-12 | Hotfix: Corrected SQL migration script for user_profiles table
       v2.4.0 | 2023-05-29 | Added support for PostgreSQL 14
       v2.3.1 | 2023-04-17 | Fixed issue with authentication timeout
       ```

    B. /home/user/deploy/logs/deployment_summary.json  
       Exact contents (one line, ending with newline):  
       ```
       {"versions":["v2.4.1","v2.4.0","v2.3.1"],"latest":"v2.4.1"}
       ```

    C. /home/user/deploy/logs/verification_report.txt  
       Exact contents (ending with newline):  
       ```
       TRANSFORMATION STATUS: SUCCESS
       ```
